Overview

Address
255 WEST BROWN ROAD, MESA, AZ 85201

Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.20 3.52 3.51 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.42 0.62 0.46 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.92 0.78 1.01 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.86 2.12 2.04 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.34 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.96
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.21
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.25
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
43.8%
annual
RN turnover
41.2%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.25
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.91
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-03-22 11 3 11 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-11-18 16 9 7 68 1 68
Total weighted health score
65.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (39)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

39 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2026-01-08 2026-02-06
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-12-02 2026-02-06
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-12-02 2026-02-06
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-12-02 2026-02-06
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-12-02 2026-02-06
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-06-18 2025-07-12
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2025-04-18 2025-06-01
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-04-18 2025-06-01
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-04-18 2025-06-01
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-04-18 2025-06-01
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-01-14 2025-03-03
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-01-14 2025-03-03
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-01-14 2025-03-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-19 2025-01-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-08-30 2024-10-04
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-05
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2024-03-01 2024-04-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-01 2024-04-26
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2022-11-18 2023-01-09
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2021-05-14 2021-06-23
